content:
# Joshua Chapter 18 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio pages 241-242
**Source Image:** `JOS_018_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_018_page_002.jpg`

---

**18:1** And the whole congregation of the Sons of Israel assembled at Shiloh, and they set up the Tent of Meeting there. And the land was subdued before them.

**18:2** And there remained among the Sons of Israel seven tribes who had not yet received their inheritance.

**18:3** And Joshua said to the Sons of Israel, "How long will you be slack in going to possess the land that YHWH, the God of your fathers, has given you?

**18:4** Appoint for yourselves three men for each tribe, and I will send them, and they shall arise and walk through the land, and they shall write a description of it according to their inheritance, and they shall come to me.

**18:5** And they shall divide it into seven portions. Judah shall stay in his territory on the south, and the house of Joseph shall stay in their territory on the north.

**18:6** And you shall write a description of the land in seven portions and bring it here to me, and I will cast lots for you here before YHWH our God.

**18:7** For the Levites have no portion among you, for the priesthood of YHWH is their inheritance. And Gad and Reuben and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance beyond the Jordan to the east, which Moses, the servant of YHWH, gave to them."

**18:8** And the men arose and went. And Joshua commanded those going to write a description of the land, saying, "Go and walk through the land and write a description of it and return to me, and here I will cast lots for you before YHWH in Shiloh."

**18:9** And the men went and passed through the land and wrote a description of it by cities in seven portions upon a scroll, and they came to Joshua at the camp in Shiloh.

**18:10** And Joshua cast lots for them in Shiloh before YHWH, and there Joshua divided the land to the Sons of Israel according to their divisions.

**18:11** And the lot of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin came up according to their clans, and the territory of their lot fell between the sons of Judah and the sons of Joseph.

**18:12** And their border on the north side was from the Jordan, and the border went up to the side of Jericho on the north, and it went up through the hill country westward, and its end was at the wilderness of Beth-aven.

**18:13** And the border passed from there to Luz, to the side of Luz — that is, Bethel — southward. And the border went down to Ataroth-addar, upon the mountain that is south of Lower Beth-horon.

**18:14** And the border was drawn and turned on the west side southward, from the mountain that is before Beth-horon southward, and its end was at Kiriath-baal — that is, Kiriath-jearim — a city of the sons of Judah. This was the west side.

**18:15** And the south side was from the edge of Kiriath-jearim, and the border went out westward, and it went out to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah.

**18:16** And the border went down to the edge of the mountain that is before the Valley of Ben-hinnom, which is in the Valley of the Rephaim northward. And it went down to the Valley of Hinnom, to the side of the Jebusite southward, and it went down to En-rogel.

**18:17** And it was drawn from the north and went out to En-shemesh, and it went out to Geliloth, which is opposite the Ascent of Adummim. And it went down to the Stone of Bohan the son of Reuben.

**18:18** And it passed to the side opposite the Arabah northward, and it went down to the Arabah.

**18:19** And the border passed to the side of Beth-hoglah northward, and the end of the border was at the tongue of the Salt Sea northward, at the south end of the Jordan. This was the south border.

**18:20** And the Jordan bordered it on the east side. This was the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin, by its borders all around, according to their clans.

**18:21** And the cities of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin according to their clans were: Jericho, and Beth-hoglah, and Emek-keziz,

**18:22** and Beth-arabah, and Zemaraim, and Bethel,

**18:23** and Avvim, and Parah, and Ophrah,

**18:24** and Chephar-ammoni, and Ophni, and Geba — twelve cities and their villages.

**18:25** Gibeon, and Ramah, and Beeroth,

**18:26** and Mizpeh, and Chephirah, and Mozah,

**18:27** and Rekem, and Irpeel, and Taralah,

**18:28** and Zela, Eleph, and the Jebusite — that is, Jerusalem — Gibeah, and Kiriath — fourteen cities and their villages. This was the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin according to their clans.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

### Method
Each verse was compared across all three translations (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA) and checked against the manuscript images of Leningrad Codex pages 241-242. Where all three translators agreed on a reading, that agreement was adopted. Where they diverged, the manuscript was consulted and the wisest synthesis was chosen, favoring accuracy to the Hebrew while maintaining readable English.

### Agreements
The three translators demonstrated strong agreement across Joshua 18. All three preserve:
- **YHWH** (not "the LORD") throughout
- **Sons of Israel** (not "Israelites") throughout
- **Tent of Meeting** for ohel mo'ed (v. 1)
- **Lots / lot** for goral throughout (vv. 6, 8, 10, 11)
- The **boundary description sequence** of Benjamin's territory (vv. 12-20) with consistent geographic terms
- The **dual place-name identifications**: Luz = Bethel (v. 13), Kiriath-baal = Kiriath-jearim (v. 14), the Jebusite = Jerusalem (v. 28)
- The **two city groups** with their counts: twelve cities (v. 24) and fourteen cities (v. 28)
- **Valley of Ben-hinnom** preserving the patronymic form (v. 16)
- **Geliloth** rather than Gilgal in v. 17, following the Leningrad Codex reading for this passage (contrast Joshua 15:7)

### Major Decisions

**1. Proper name rendering: anglicized forms (throughout)**
EMET uses Hebrew transliterations (Yehoshua, Yisrael, Yarden, Yericho, Binyamin, Yehudah, Yosef, Menasheh, Moshe, etc.) while LOGOS and PNEUMA use anglicized forms (Joshua, Israel, Jordan, Jericho, Benjamin, Judah, Joseph, Manasseh, Moses). The SOPHIA consensus adopts anglicized forms, consistent with our prior ruling in Joshua 4. These proper nouns have universally established English equivalents, unlike the divine name YHWH. See Disagreement Log.

**2. "Set up" vs. "caused to dwell" for vayyashkinu (v. 1)**
EMET renders "caused the tent of meeting to dwell there," preserving the Hiphil causative of shakhan. LOGOS renders "set up the Tent of Meeting there." PNEUMA renders "set up the Tent of Meeting there." SOPHIA adopts "set up" (LOGOS, PNEUMA). While EMET's rendering faithfully preserves the Hiphil stem, "caused to dwell" applied to a tent is awkward in English. The verb shakhan in the Hiphil with the Tabernacle as object means to establish its residence — "set up" captures this. The theological resonance of shakhan (the root of Shekhinah, "divine dwelling presence") is noted in the council notes rather than forced into the translation. See Disagreement Log.

**3. "How long will you be slack" vs. "How long are you going to put off" (v. 3)**
EMET renders "Until when [will] you [be] slack," LOGOS renders "How long will you be slack," PNEUMA renders "How long are you going to put off going in." SOPHIA adopts "How long will you be slack" (LOGOS), which preserves the force of the Hebrew Hitpael participle mitrappim (from raphah, "to be slack, lax") while being comprehensible English. PNEUMA's "put off" captures the procrastination nuance but loses the specific Hebrew word's connotation of physical laxness. EMET's "Until when" is overly literal for the Hebrew ad-anah. See Disagreement Log.

**4. "Walk through" vs. "walk about" (vv. 4, 8)**
EMET renders "walk about" (hithalekhu), LOGOS renders "walk through," PNEUMA renders "go through" / "walk through." The Hebrew root halakh in the Hitpael means "to walk about, traverse," suggesting thorough movement throughout an area. SOPHIA adopts "walk through" (LOGOS, PNEUMA), which conveys purposeful traversal for the sake of the survey, while "walk about" (EMET) might suggest aimless wandering. See Disagreement Log.

**5. "Write a description" vs. "write it" (vv. 4, 6, 8, 9)**
EMET renders simply "write it" / "write the land," staying close to the terse Hebrew. LOGOS and PNEUMA both supply "write a description of it," making explicit what is implicit in the Hebrew — that the writing was a survey description. SOPHIA adopts "write a description of it" (LOGOS, PNEUMA) for clarity, since in English "write the land" is opaque. The Hebrew verb katav with the land as object implies recording a description, and making this explicit aids comprehension without distorting meaning. See Disagreement Log.

**6. "Tongue of the Salt Sea" vs. "northern bay of the Salt Sea" (v. 19)**
EMET renders "the tongue of the Sea of the Salt" (preserving the Hebrew leshon, "tongue"), LOGOS renders "the northern bay of the Salt Sea" (interpreting leshon as "bay"), PNEUMA renders "the northern bay of the Salt Sea." SOPHIA adopts "the tongue of the Salt Sea" — preserving the Hebrew geographic metaphor leshon ("tongue"), which describes a protruding inlet. This is a characteristic biblical land-description term, and "tongue" is comprehensible as a geographic feature in English. The modifier "northward" in the Hebrew is retained to specify direction. See Disagreement Log.

**7. "Goings out" vs. "end" vs. "ended at" for totz'otav (vv. 12, 14, 19)**
EMET renders "its goings out were" (preserving the Hebrew totz'ot, plural of "going out"), LOGOS renders "its end was at," PNEUMA renders "ended at." SOPHIA adopts "its end was at" (LOGOS) in vv. 12 and 14, and "the end of the border was at" in v. 19. While EMET's "goings out" is etymologically precise, the Hebrew totz'ot in boundary descriptions is a technical term meaning the terminus or endpoint of a border. "End" captures this meaning clearly. See Disagreement Log.

**8. "Fell between" vs. "went out between" for the lot boundary (v. 11)**
EMET renders "the border of their lot went out between," LOGOS renders "the territory of their lot fell between," PNEUMA renders "their allotted territory fell between." The Hebrew vayyetze can mean "went out" but in the context of lot-casting the sense is that the lot "came forth" to assign territory that lay between Judah and Joseph. SOPHIA adopts "the territory of their lot fell between" (LOGOS), using "territory" for gvul (which can mean border or territory) and "fell" to convey the lot-determined assignment. See Disagreement Log.

**9. Hebrew article in city names: ha- prefix (vv. 23-28)**
EMET consistently preserves the Hebrew definite article as "ha-" in city names (ha-Avvim, ha-Parah, ha-Ramah, ha-Mitzpeh, ha-Kephirah, ha-Motzah, ha-Eleph, ha-Yevusi). LOGOS and PNEUMA drop the article, rendering simply "Avvim, Parah, Ramah, Mizpeh, Chephirah, Mozah, Eleph, the Jebusite." SOPHIA follows LOGOS and PNEUMA in dropping the ha- prefix for city names, as these are established proper nouns in English biblical convention. The one exception is "the Jebusite" (ha-Yevusi) in v. 28, where all three translators retain the article because it functions as a gentilic designation identifying the pre-Israelite inhabitants, not merely a place name. See Disagreement Log.

**10. "Upon a scroll" vs. "in a scroll" (v. 9)**
EMET renders "upon a scroll," LOGOS renders "in a scroll," PNEUMA renders "on a scroll." The Hebrew is al-sepher. SOPHIA adopts "upon a scroll," preserving the Hebrew preposition al ("upon, on") and the sense that the description was inscribed on a physical writing surface. See Disagreement Log.

**11. City count in v. 28**
All three translators preserve the Masoretic count of "fourteen cities" despite the apparent discrepancy when counting the listed names. All three note this as a longstanding textual question. SOPHIA preserves the text as written in the Leningrad Codex without emendation. The Masoretic punctuation may support reading "Zela-Eleph" as a single compound name and "Gibeah-Kiriath" as another, which would resolve the count to fourteen. This matter is noted but not resolved by emendation — the SOPHIA text follows the received text.

### Manuscript Observations
The Leningrad Codex manuscript images (pages 241-242) confirm the text of Joshua 18 as represented in the three translations. Key observations:
- The three-column layout of the Codex is clearly visible, with Masoretic marginalia and verse markers.
- The text transitions from the end of Joshua 17 into Joshua 18 on page 241.
- The boundary descriptions and city lists continue through page 242.
- No significant scribal corrections, erasures, or interlinear glosses are visible that would affect the translation of this chapter.
- The Codex reading of Geliloth (v. 17) is confirmed, distinct from the Gilgal reading in the parallel passage of Joshua 15:7.

---
